Rewards Credit Card Benefits
4X Rewards
4 reward points* per INR 150 on all Retail spends (except Government, Insurance and Fuel categories)
Bonus Rewards
Additional 4 reward points per INR 150 on monthly Retail spends* above INR 20,000 (max. 2000 bonus reward points per statement cycle)
First Year Free Card
No joining fees. 2nd Year onwards the annual fees to be reversed on spends of INR 1,50,000 or more in the previous year. Alternatively, client will be entitled to receive 4000 reward points* on payment of Annual Fees.
Complimentary Lounge Access
1 Domestic Lounge Visit* per calendar quarter (4 per year)
1X rewards
1 reward point on every INR 150 spent on Government and Insurance categories
Fuel Surcharge Waiver
1% Fuel Surcharge waiver on all fuel spends*

Rewards Credit Card is offered on Mastercard Network

Other Credit Card Features
Supplementary Credit Card
Extend the credit card benefits with your family member. Spread the joy with them!
Contactless Credit Card
Use contactless credit card & enjoy complete freedom cash transaction with faster checkouts & secure payments.
Secure Online Transactions
Enjoy enhanced security for online transactions on your credit card through 3D secure OTP Verification.
Digital Payment Solutions
Avail wide range of instant payment solutions like BharatQR, Bharat Bill Payment Solution (BBPS) & Samsung Secure Pay.
Kuch Bhi on EMI
With Kuch bhi on EMI option, convert your big spends above ₹ 2,000 to easy EMI through online banking or SC Mobile. Get attractive pricing & flexible tenure options.
360° Rewards
With Standard Chartered 360° Rewards, you can easily redeem your rewards points against our new online catalogue.
Dial-a-loan
Get loan up to ₹ 5,00,000 on your credit card with flexible tenure from 12 to 60 months.
Balance Transfer
Transfer card balance from other bank credit cards up to ₹ 5,00,000 at attractive interest rate.
Balance EMI
Convert your entire outstanding (up to ₹ 5,00,000) in EMIs with zero processing fees.
Eligibility & Documents
Eligibility
Documents
Age
Applicant’s age should be between 18 and 65 years.
Minimum age for add-on cardholder is 18 years.
Monthly Income
Applicants should have a stable monthly income.
Other Requirements
Applicants should belong to credit card sourcing cities/locations of the Bank.
All applications are subject to credit and other policy checks of the Bank.
You will need
Identity Proof (Any One of the following Photo cards): Passport, Voter Id Card, Govt ID, etc..
PAN Card
Address Proof (Any One of the following documents with current residence address): Aadhaar Card, Passport, Driving License, Election/Voter’s ID Card, Job card issued by NREGA duly signed by an officer of the State Government, The letter issued by the National Population Register containing details of name, address.
1 recent passport-sized Photograph
Financial documents for salaried
Latest one month salary slip
Financial documents for self employed
Self Employed Businessmen: Latest IT Returns with computation of income /Certified Financials, Last Business Continuity proof.
Self Employed Professionals: Latest IT Returns, Business Continuity proof.
Fees & Charges
Joining Fee
No Joining Fee
Annual Fees
INR 1000 + GST
Renewal fees will be reversed on spends of INR 1,50,000 or higher in previous year using Rewards Credit Card
Rewards Credit Card FAQs
I already have an existing Standard Chartered Platinum Rewards credit card, can I also apply for a Reward credit card?
Yes, existing credit cardholders can also apply for the Rewards Credit Card by authenticating their existing card with an INR 1 transaction via sc.com/in
What is the Joining/annual fees for the Rewards Credit Card?
Joining Fee is NIL . Annual Fee for the Rewards Credit Card is INR 1000 +GST with a fee reversal on spends of INR 1,50,000 in the previous year
How will I receive the 4000 reward points on payment of annual fees?
Client is entitled to receive 4000 reward points within 60 days of realization of fees by the Bank. Please note that client will receive the mentioned reward points only if he/she does not receive the spend-based annual fee reversal.
What are categories that I will earn reward points on?
Transactions
Reward Points
Every INR 150 spent on Retail transactions
4 Reward Points
Every INR 150 spent on Government and Insurance categories
1 Reward Point
How do I avail the Bonus reward points?
Bonus reward points are applicable on achieving monthly retail spends of INR 20,000 and above in all categories, except Government and Insurance payments. A client can earn additional 4 reward points per INR 150* spent on Retail categories* for monthly spends above INR 20,000.
Will I earn reward points on Fuel transactions using the Rewards Credit Card?
Fuel transactions are not eligible for reward points.
Will I earn reward points on cash withdrawal transactions?
Cash withdrawal transactions are not eligible for reward points.
What is the value of a reward point on Rewards Credit Card?
1 RP = INR 0.25 (for redemption from Rewards catalogue)
1 RP = INR 0.20 (for adjustment against Statement outstanding balance)
Can reward points earned be redeemed against an outstanding credit card statement?
Yes, a minimum of 1000 Reward points is required to redeem.
Is there any capping of reward points on the Rewards Credit Card?
There is no cap on the base reward points. However, the bonus reward points (i.e. on spends above INR 20,000) are capped at 2000 points.
Where can I see the reward points that I have earned on my Rewards credit card?
Client can view his/her Reward Points on the 360 Rewards portal. To view, log in to Online Banking and proceed to the 360 Rewards portal or log in directly: https://retail.sc.com/in/rewards360/rewards_home/in
When can I see reward points earned on Rewards credit card?
Reward points earned on transactions done using the Rewards Credit Card prior to the statement generation date will reflect on the same month’s statement and on the 360 Rewards portal. Bonus reward points earned for a statement cycle will be credited within 2-3 days from statement generation date.
Is any Redemption Fee applicable on redeeming reward points?
Yes, there is a redemption fee of INR 99/- (Rupees Ninety-Nine Only) on redeeming reward points.
Will I earn reward points on transactions converted into EMI?
Yes, clients can earn reward points on transactions converted into EMI.
What is the minimum transaction and capping for Fuel Surcharge waiver in a month?
The minimum transaction amount is INR 400. The maximum transaction amount is INR 2000. Fuel Surcharge Waiver amount is capped at INR 400 per month.
What is the complimentary lounge access offer?
With the Rewards Credit Card, client can avail 1 complimentary domestic airport lounge visit per quarter.
What merchant categories will be considered as rental payments?
Credit card transactions with Merchant Category Code (MCC) 6513 are covered under Rental Payments.
A Merchant Category Code (MCC) is a four-digit number assigned to a merchant/business by the merchant’s acquiring bank. These are defined by networks e.g. Visa, MasterCard.
What is the processing fee for rental payments?
A processing fee of 1% (plus taxes) would be applicable to all rental transactions. This fee will be over and above any charges applied by the merchant as communicated to the client at the time of transaction.
For example – If you pay INR 20,000 on a third-party rent payment platform using your Standard Chartered Bank Credit Card, 1% of transaction amount i.e. INR 200 plus taxes will be charged as a processing fee
When will this processing fee be effective from?
Processing fee on rental payments will be effective from April 02, 2023.
When will this fee get levied?
The processing fee will be levied by 15 th  of the next calendar month and will have a separate narration in the monthly statement.

Ultimate Credit Card FAQs
What are the features of Ultimate Credit Card?
Joining fee = Rs 5,000
Renewal fee = Rs 5,000
Complimentary membership to Priority Pass to primary card holder as well as supplementary card holder. Usage is chargeable at 27 USD per visit. The fees will be waived for 1 visit per month for customers whose card spend in the last month exceeds INR 20,000
Travel and Air Accident Cover up to ($ 200K).& Medical Travel Insurance up to $ 25K.
Cashback of 5%* at duty-free store
1%* fuel surcharge waiver
Exclusive Golf privileges
What is the annual Fee on the same?
Rs 5,000 to be paid every year
Is there any change in Forex Markup Fees levied on foreign transactions using my Ultimate Credit Card?
W.e.f 25-Aug-2024, all overseas transactions will be levied with 2% forex markup fee at the time of transaction posting itself on the Ultimate Credit Card. This will eliminate the hassle of waiting for a separate posting of 1.5% cashback.
I am already on the free version of the Visa Infinite card- can I upgrade to the Ultimate credit card?
Yes, you can, please call Phone Banking for the same.
What is the age limit for availing medical insurance?
Cardholder to be between 18-70 years of age. Also this facility is available only for the primary cardholder
What is the condition for free lounge access ?
The domestic lounge program under VISA/Mastercard provides you 4 domestic lounge accesses per calendar quarter using your Ultimate Credit Card. This is complimentary for all cardholders.
The Priority Pass programme provides you with 1 international lounge visit per month, subject to the cardholder having spent more than INR 20,000 in the previous calendar month of the visit. Please note that the card member will be liable to pay for each domestic lounge visit using Priority Pass, as per applicable charges.
Can I apply for an Ultimate virtual card?
Yes, you can apply for an Ultimate virtual credit card for golf benefits, dining privileges & more & access these perks instantly To know more, click here .
How to access Priority Pass Lounge Benefits?
Your Ultimate credit card offers you complimentary membership to Priority Pass, which gives you access to lounges in international airports across the globe. You can log on to www.prioritypass.com for details on the participating lounges. To know more on the terms and conditions, click here .
What merchant categories will be considered as rental payments?
Credit card transactions with Merchant Category Code (MCC) 6513 are covered under Rental Payments.
A Merchant Category Code (MCC) is a four-digit number assigned to a merchant/business by the merchant’s acquiring bank. These are defined by networks e.g. Visa, MasterCard.
What is the processing fee for rental payments?
A processing fee of 1% (plus taxes) would be applicable to all rental transactions. This fee will be over and above any charges applied by the merchant as communicated to the client at the time of transaction.
For example – If you pay INR 20,000 on a third-party rent payment platform using your Standard Chartered Bank Credit Card, 1% of transaction amount i.e. INR 200 plus taxes will be charged as a processing fee
When will this processing fee be effective from?
Processing fee on rental payments will be effective from April 02, 2023.
When will this fee get levied?
The processing fee will be levied by 15 th  of the next calendar month and will have a separate narration in the monthly statement.
Which are the merchant categories selected for reward point revision?
From April 2 nd , 2023, transactions on Ultimate credit card for Utilities, Supermarkets, Insurance, Property management, Schools & Government payments will now earn 3 reward points on every INR150 spent.

Is there any capping to the reward points earned on merchant categories with revision?
Please note there is no capping to the reward points that can be earned in a month on the merchant categories where there is a revision in reward points.
How many reward points will I earn on fuel transactions using Ultimate credit card?
From April 2 nd , 2023, no reward points will be earned for Fuel transactions on StanChart Ultimate credit card.
However, clients will now enjoy a surcharge waiver of 1% on fuel spends across all petrol pumps. The fuel surcharge waiver amount will be capped at Max Rs 1000/ per month.
When will the changes be effective from?
The changes would be effective from April 2 nd , 2023. Please note all spends till 31 st  March 2023 on the select categories will continue to earn reward points as per the existing reward proposition i.e. 5 reward points on every INR 150 spent.
Will there be any impact to my existing reward points?
There will be no impact to your existing reward points which you have earned and is available for redemption till expiry of those points.
What is the Air Accident Insurance feature on my Ultimate Credit Card?

Platinum Rewards Credit Card FAQs
Can I apply for a Platinum Rewards virtual card?
Yes, you can apply for a virtual card for the Platinum Rewards credit card. Once your credit card is approved, you can avail of and use your virtual card details. To know more, click here .
How to apply for a Platinum Rewards credit card?
You can easily apply for the Platinum Rewards credit card through the following steps:
● Click here to go to the online application page.
● Fill in the required details and click ‘Proceed’.
● Fill the application form and submit your application.
Once your application form is submitted, you will be contacted by our official in-charge and your documents will be verified. You will receive your credit card after the verification process is completed.
What are the main benefits of a Platinum Rewards credit card?
The main benefits of Platinum Rewards credit card include the following:
● Earn 5 reward points for every INR 150 spent on dining.
● Earn 5 reward points for every INR 150 spent on fuel.
● Earn 1 reward point for every INR 150 spent on all other categories.
● Exclusive offers and discounts across shopping, travel, dining and more.
What are reward points?
Reward points are one of the incentives banks provide to its customers when they use their credit cards. With Standard Chartered’s Platinum Rewards credit card, you can earn reward points for every INR 150 you spend to make a purchase, either online or offline, making it the best credit card for the same. As you gain more reward points, you can redeem them for a wide variety of vouchers, gift cards and much more.
How do I earn reward points with a Platinum Rewards credit card?
When you shop with a Platinum Rewards credit card, you are bound to earn reward points on almost anything you purchase. For dining and fuel expenditure you gain accelerated rewards, which comes to about 5x reward points for every INR 150 you spend. For every other category, you can earn 1x reward points for the same amount spent on purchases.
How to redeem reward points?
● Online – Login to Standard Chartered netbanking portal and choose options such as gift vouchers or retail items, for which you can redeem your reward points.
● Customer care – Contact our customer care service and gain assistance with the reward redemption process.
What are the Fee options for Platinum Rewards credit card?
The Platinum Rewards credit card comes with comfortable fee rates. In the first year, you are required to pay only INR 250. The renewal fee, which commences from the 2nd year onwards, is also INR 250.
Is there any additional fee on Supplementary cards for the Platinum Rewards card?
While there is a first year and renewal fee for the Platinum Rewards credit card, this is not a requirement for its supplementary cardholders. Joining offers are also not applicable for supplementary cards.
Can I get a loan on Platinum Rewards card?
The Standard Chartered Dial-a-Loan feature is available for Platinum Rewards credit card users. You can avail loan amounts* up to INR 5,00,000 and enjoy a flexible tenure on your repayment, ranging between 12 and 60 months.
Do I get lifetime rewards on my card?
You can earn 5x reward points on every INR 150 spent on dining or on fuel expenditure as well as 1x reward points on the same amount for purchases made in all other categories with no monthly limit to your reward points.
What are the important things to remember about reward points?
● You will want to redeem your reward points and vouchers before their expiry dates.
● Vouchers may cost more than their actual worth in order to be redeemable.
What are the benefits of credit card reward points?
Reward points can enhance your lifestyle in the following ways:
● You can use your reward points to book flights or hotel stays.
● You can redeem points with vouchers and items from assorted catalogues.
What is VKYC?
VKYC, or simply Video KYC, is a feature that allows you to complete your KYC through a video from the comfort of your home while applying online for a Standard Chartered credit card, personal loan, or savings account. With this feature, our application process becomes even more simpler and completely contactless. Click here to learn more.
What merchant categories will be considered as rental payments?
Credit card transactions with Merchant Category Code (MCC) 6513 are covered under Rental Payments.
A Merchant Category Code (MCC) is a four-digit number assigned to a merchant/business by the merchant’s acquiring bank. These are defined by networks e.g. Visa, MasterCard.
What is the processing fee for rental payments?
A processing fee of 1% (plus taxes) would be applicable to all rental transactions. This fee will be over and above any charges applied by the merchant as communicated to the client at the time of transaction.
For example – If you pay INR 20,000 on a third-party rent payment platform using your Standard Chartered Bank Credit Card, 1% of transaction amount i.e. INR 200 plus taxes will be charged as a processing fee
When will this processing fee be effective from?
Processing fee on rental payments will be effective from April 02, 2023.
When will this fee get levied?
The processing fee will be levied by 15 th  of the next calendar month and will have a separate narration in the monthly statement.
What is the One-time Fee and get levied?
One Time of INR 250 + GST will be levied if the client doesn’t make a retail transaction of INR 500 or more within 30 days from card issuance. One-time fee is charged in subsequent statement after 60 days completion of Card Set up Month. Example: If the card is set up month is Jan 23, then one time would be charged in statement of April 23.

Super Value Titanium Credit Card FAQs
Can I pay for fuel using my credit card?
There’s a reason why Standard Chartered’s Super Value Titanium is the best credit card for fuel expenditure; after all, you can pay for fuel at petrol pumps across India and earn 5 % cashback up to INR 200 per month!
What is a fuel credit card and is a Super Value Titanium card a fuel credit card?
A fuel credit card is specially designed to offer you benefits when used for purchasing fuel. The Super Value Titanium card is ideal for those who require to spend a lot per month on fuel purchases. It also offers benefits on other expenditure, in the form of cashbacks and reward points.
What is changing with respect to the cashback benefits on fuel on my Super Value Titanium Credit Card?
W.e.f 25-Aug-2024, 1% fuel surcharge waiver on your Super Value Titanium Credit Card will be discontinued. The existing feature of 4% cashback on fuel spends will now be increased to 5%, with a monthly cap of INR 200 and credited to the R360 portal.
What bills can be paid with a Super Value Titanium card?
The Super Value Titanium credit card can be used for any kind of purchase. However, it is best suited to pay your fuel, telephone and utility bills because of the cashback offers we provide when used for the same. You also earn 1 reward point on every other purchase, redeemable against our new catalogue with exciting discounts and vouchers.
Can I pay the invoice with my card?
While the Super Value Titanium credit card is best appreciated as a fuel credit card, it can also be used for other purchases including bills for telephone and utility purposes.
Can I get an add-on card? And will there be a separate credit card limit on the add-on card?
Yes, you can apply for an add-on card. There will not be a separate credit card limit for the add-on card.
Will I be charged anything extra if I use my credit card overseas?
Unfortunately, yes. All overseas transactions are levied with a 3.5% transaction fee.
What are the benefits of the Standard Chartered cashback credit card?
This cashback credit card provides multiple benefits, such as:
● 5% cashback on telephone bills
● 5% cashback on utility bills
● 5% cashback on fuel
● Reward point for every INR 150 spent using your credit card
● Complimentary Priority Pass providing access to 1000+ airport lounges across the globe for Premium Banking Customers
How to apply for the cashback credit card online?
You can apply online for the cashback credit card and get an instant in-principle decision regarding its approval. Once you have submitted your application online, our bank representative will get in touch with you to complete the documentation requirements. From there on out, your card will be subject to final policy and verification checks of the bank and you should be able to receive it within 7-15 days from the time you submit your documents and the signed application form. Although it may take longer in some cases.
On what other spends will the credit cardholder earn reward points?
As a Super Value Titanium credit cardholder, you will receive cashback on fuel, utility and phone bills. For all other spends you will earn reward points.
Will the credit cardholder receive cashback and reward points on making payments from any bill pay platform?
Super Value Titanium credit cardholders are requested to use the bill pay platforms of Standard Chartered Bank to get cashback on utility and telephone bills.
What is a Virtual Super Value Titanium credit card and how to get one?
A virtual Super Value Titanium credit card is the online replication of the physical card with a unique card number, CVV and expiry date. It can be used instantly from anywhere across the globe without the need of the physical card – you can simply store the virtual card details on your smartphone.
On issuing a Super Value Titanium credit card, you can easily apply for its virtual card too with a few, uncomplicated select-and-apply steps. On completing the process, you can access your virtual credit card for online shopping immediately with zero waiting time. To know more about the application process, click here .
What is VKYC?
VKYC, or simply Video KYC, is a feature that allows you to complete your KYC through a video from the comfort of your home while applying online for a Standard Chartered credit card, personal loan, or savings account. With this feature, our application process becomes even more simpler and completely contactless. Click here to learn more
What merchant categories will be considered as rental payments?
Credit card transactions with Merchant Category Code (MCC) 6513 are covered under Rental Payments.
A Merchant Category Code (MCC) is a four-digit number assigned to a merchant/business by the merchant’s acquiring bank. These are defined by networks e.g. Visa, MasterCard.
What is the processing fee for rental payments?
A processing fee of 1% (plus taxes) would be applicable to all rental transactions. This fee will be over and above any charges applied by the merchant as communicated to the client at the time of transaction.
For example – If you pay INR 20,000 on a third-party rent payment platform using your Standard Chartered Bank Credit Card, 1% of transaction amount i.e. INR 200 plus taxes will be charged as a processing fee
When will this processing fee be effective from?
Processing fee on rental payments will be effective from April 02, 2023.
When will this fee get levied?
The processing fee will be levied by 15 th  of the next calendar month and will have a separate narration in the monthly statement.
I am a Premium Banking client holding a Premium Super Value Titanium Credit Card. What lounge access features are offered on my Credit Card?
Your Premium Super Value Titanium Credit Card makes you eligible for 1 complimentary* international lounge access (outside India) per month using Priority Pass FUM qualification of INR 15 lakhs at an individual account level or having monthly salary credit of INR 1.5 lakhs or above basis Premium Banking programme qualification criteria. There is no domestic lounge access on your credit card.

Manhattan Platinum Credit Card FAQs
How long does it take to apply for a credit card?
Provided with all the document(s) required, it should take approximately 6 days to get your credit card approved.
What are my obligations to hold a credit card?
The minimum amount due every month shall be higher of the following:
(a) 5% of statement outstanding or
(b) Sum total of all instalments billed, interest, fees, other charges, amount that is over limit and 1% of the principal or
(c) Rs 250
In case you have an unpaid minimum amount due from the previous month, the same will be added to the minimum amount due for the current month (as calculated above).
Will I be charged anything extra if I use my card overseas?
All overseas transactions are levied with a 3.5% transaction fee. This includes charges paid out to Visa / Mastercard for converting overseas transactions into INR. Pl note: transactions at merchant establishments that are registered overseas even if the merchant is located in India attract a charge of 1% from Visa / Mastercard which is included as a part of the transaction amount in your statement.
How much is the annual Government Service Tax?
Goods and Services Tax (GST) will be levied at the applicable rates in force on all taxable supplies with effect from 1 July 2017.
Late Payment Charges
₹ 0 for statement outstanding balance less than or equal to ₹ 100. ₹ 100 for statement outstanding balance between ₹ 101 and ₹ 500. ₹ 500 for statement outstanding balance between ₹ 501 and ₹ 5,000. ₹ 700 for statement outstanding balance between ₹ 5,001 and ₹ 10,000. ₹ 800 for statement outstanding balance between ₹ 10,000 and ₹ 25,000. ₹ 1200 for statement outstanding balance greater than ₹ 25,000.
Example: In case there is an outstanding of ₹ 4,000 on your credit card and ₹ 15,000 in your Balance Transfer account and payment is not made by the Payment Due Date. There will be a Late Payment Charge of ₹ 500 in your credit card account and ₹ 800 on your Balance Transfer account. The example above is for illustrative purposes only. All cards and all instalment products such as Instabuy, Balance Transfer, Dial-a-Loan etc. will each attract Late Payment Charges if payment is not made by the Payment Due Date.
How much is the interest charges?
The monthly interest rate is annualized to arrive at the annualized percentage rate (APR). Monthly interest rate of 3.75% pm is annualized to arrive at an APR of 45.00% for all Standard Chartered Credit Cards including Visa Infinite and Emirates World credit cards. However, for all instant credit card variants, the monthly interest rate is 1.99% pm (APR of 23.88%). Cash transactions will also attract an interest rate of 3.75% pm (APR of 45.00%).

Annualized Percentage Rate (w.e.f. 1 May 2021):
The monthly interest rate is annualized to arrive at the annualized percentage rate (APR).
Monthly interest rate of 3.75% pm is annualized to arrive at an APR of 45.00% for all Standard Chartered Credit Cards including Visa Infinite and Emirates World credit cards.
However, for all instant credit card variants, the monthly interest rate is 1.99% pm (APR of 23.88%).
Cash transactions will also attract an interest rate of 3.75% pm (APR of 45.00%).
ATM withdrawals:
INR 100 for statement outstanding balance less than or equal to INR 500.
INR 350 for statement outstanding balance between INR 501 and INR 2,000.
INR 500 for statement outstanding balance between INR 2,001 and INR 4,000.
INR 700 for statement outstanding balance between INR 4,001 and INR 20,000.
INR 800 for statement outstanding balance greater than INR 20,000.

You will need

Priority Visa Infinite Credit Card FAQs
How long does it take to apply for a credit card?
Subject to documents being complete and accurate, it should take approximately 9 days to get your credit card approved.
What are my obligations to hold a credit card?
The minimum amount due every month shall be higher than the following:
(a) 5% of statement outstanding or
(b) Sum total of all installments billed, interest, fees, other charges, amount that is over limit and 1% of the principal or
(c) INR 250
In case you have an unpaid minimum amount due from the previous month, the same will be added to the minimum amount due for the current month (as calculated above).
Will I be charged anything extra if I use my card overseas?
All overseas transactions are levied with a 3.5% transaction fee.
This includes charges paid out to Visa / Mastercard for converting overseas transactions into INR.
Please note that the transactions at merchant establishments that are registered overseas even if the merchant is located in India attract a charge of 1% from Visa / Mastercard which is included as a part of the transaction amount in your statement.
What is the tax applicable ?
Goods and Services Tax (GST) will be levied at the applicable rates in force on all taxable supplies with effect from 1 July 2017.
Late Payment Charges
₹ 0 for statement outstanding balance less than or equal to ₹ 100. ₹ 100 for statement outstanding balance between ₹ 101 and ₹ 500. ₹ 500 for statement outstanding balance between ₹ 501 and ₹ 5,000. ₹ 700 for statement outstanding balance between ₹ 5,001 and ₹ 10,000. ₹ 800 for statement outstanding balance between ₹ 10,000 and ₹ 25,000. ₹ 1200 for statement outstanding balance greater than ₹ 25,000.
Example: In case there is an outstanding of ₹ 4,000 on your credit card and ₹ 15,000 in your Balance Transfer account and payment is not made by the Payment Due Date. There will be a Late Payment Charge of ₹ 500 in your credit card account and ₹ 800 on your Balance Transfer account. The example above is for illustrative purposes only. All cards and all instalment products such as Instabuy, Balance Transfer, Dial-a-Loan etc. will each attract Late Payment Charges if payment is not made by the Payment Due Date.
How much is the interest charges?
The monthly interest rate is annualized to arrive at the annualized percentage rate (APR).
Monthly interest rate of 3.75% pm is annualized to arrive at an APR of 45.00% for all Standard Chartered Credit Cards including Visa Infinite and Emirates World credit cards.
However, for all instant credit card variants, the monthly interest rate is 1.99% pm (APR of 23.88%).
Cash transactions will also attract an interest rate of 3.75% pm (APR of 45.00%).
How do I report that my card has been lost or stolen? or If I suspect someone has stolen my password or used it to make a fraudulent purchase, what should I do?
You must notify us immediately if you become aware that your card has been lost, stolen or misused.

How can I make payments on my credit card account?
Bill Desk
From different bank accounts directly to your card account. Click  here for more details.
NEFT / IBFT
From your bank account directly to your card account by quoting the IFSC code SCBL0036001 and the address as MG Road, Mumbai.
Visa money transfer
In case of Visa franchisee credit cards, pay through your bank account using Visa money transfer.
Fees for Visa money transfer may be levied by the initiating bank.
Standard Chartered online banking
Standard Chartered account holders can pay through an account transfer.
NACH
National Automated Clearing House facilitates standing instructions from your account to the credit card.
This can be initiated by submitting an NACH form authorizing transfer of funds.
This form needs to be attested by the bank from where the payment needs to be made.
You are responsible for ensuring that the debit instruction is honored.
Cheque / Draft payment
Dropping a cheque or a draft in favour of your Standard Chartered bank card no. xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx (your 16 digit card number) into any of our cheque collection boxes.
Visit here  for the complete list of the locations of cheque collection boxes.
The cheque or draft should be complete in all respects. Any material alteration must be duly counter signed.
Make your payments at least 3 days in advance of your payment due date to facilitate the timely credit of the funds into your card account. Note that your credit limit will only be increased by the amount you have repaid after our receipt of your funds. In case we do not receive payment by the payment due date, we reserve the right to levy interest and late payment charges. If you hold multiple cards or EMI accounts with us, please give us clear instructions on the allocation of payment to these various card accounts on the reverse of your cheques. If you are issuing separate cheques for each card account, then you should clearly specify not to allocate on the reverse of the cheque.In the absence of any specific information on this front, we will apply the funds first towards clearance of the minimum amount due in respect of all card accounts you hold with us.Thereafter, the excess payment will be allocated sequentially towards payment of the card account with highest balance.If any of your card account is overdue, we reserve the right to prioritize payments to overdue accounts first as per our internal policy.For cash payments, funds transfer and payment through online banking, individual payment should be made against each of the accounts.
Cash
Cash payments can only be deposited at our branches using teller facilities. INR 100 will be charged per payment transaction for card payment made by depositing cash at any Standard Chartered branch. The same will reflect in the next month’s statement.
What is Visa credit card payment service?
Visa credit card payment allows you to transfer funds from our existing Standard Chartered account to any Visa credit card within India.
How does one access the Visa credit card payment service?
To access this service, click here and just login to online banking.
Where can I transfer funds?
You can transfer funds to any Visa credit card across India.
Can I pay my Visa credit card bills using Visa credit card payment?
Yes, you can pay your Visa credit card bills using Visa credit card payment.
Just enter the card number that you wish to make a payment in the beneficiary card details.
Can I pay my Mastercard credit card bills using Visa credit card payment?
No, you cannot pay your Mastercard credit card bills using Visa credit card payment.
How long does it take to credit funds to the beneficiary?
Funds are usually credited within 3-4 working days from the date of receipt of the request.
Do I need to register for this service?
No , but  you need to be a registered user of online banking. There is no separate registration to use the Visa credit card payment service.
What details do I need to know about the beneficiary to use the service?
You only need to know the 16 digit credit card number of the beneficiary card.
Will I be charged for using the facility?
Currently there are no charges levied on this service.
Is there any per day limit to transfer funds?
Yes, there is a Minimum Limit of INR 100/- and a Maximum Limit of INR 49,999/- per transfer. The per day Transaction Limit is INR 100,000.
Once the transfer has been initiated, can it be modified / stopped?
No, once the transfer has been initiated neither can it be stopped nor can the beneficiary card details or transferred amount be changed.
Can a credit card be used to transfer funds through Visa credit card payment?
No, only resident bank accounts can be used to transfer funds.
How do you use your credit card at an online merchant store?
At the merchant site, enter your card details.
You will then be directed to a Standard Chartered security page where you will be required to enter the OTP (One Time Password) that is sent to your registered mobile number and registered email ID.
Enter the OTP and complete the transaction.
Please note that the OTP will appear in lieu of the verified by Visa / Mastercard secure password.
What if I do not receive the OTP?
In case you do not receive the OTP within 30 seconds, you can request for another OTP by pressing the “Resend OTP” number.
If you do not receive the OTP after multiple attempts, please call our phone banking numbers to ensure your mobile number is registered with us.
Can I apply for an Platinum Rewards virtual card?
Yes, you can apply for a virtual card for Platinum Rewards credit card. To know more, click here .
What are the Fee options for Platinum Rewards credit card?
The first year fee is INR 250 and the renewal fee (from 2nd year onwards) is also INR 250.
What are the various Fee offers for Platinum Rewards credit card?
100% cashback upto INR 500 is applicable on Restaurant spends in the first 90 days. Whereas, renewal fee to be reversed on spending INR 60,000 in the Year.
Is there any additional fee on Supplementary cards for Platinum Rewards card?
No, supplementary cards do not attract neither a first year fee nor a renewal fee. Joining offers are also not applicable to Supplementary Cards.
What merchant categories will be considered as rental payments?
Credit card transactions with Merchant Category Code (MCC) 6513 are covered under Rental Payments.
A Merchant Category Code (MCC) is a four-digit number assigned to a merchant/business by the merchant’s acquiring bank. These are defined by networks e.g. Visa, MasterCard.
What is the processing fee for rental payments?
A processing fee of 1% (plus taxes) would be applicable to all rental transactions. This fee will be over and above any charges applied by the merchant as communicated to the client at the time of transaction.
For example – If you pay INR 20,000 on a third-party rent payment platform using your Standard Chartered Bank Credit Card, 1% of transaction amount i.e. INR 200 plus taxes will be charged as a processing fee
When will this processing fee be effective from?
Processing fee on rental payments will be effective from April 02, 2023.
When will this fee get levied?
The processing fee will be levied by 15 th  of the next calendar month and will have a separate narration in the monthly statement.
What is the condition for free lounge access?
The domestic lounge program under VISA provides you 4 domestic lounge accesses per calendar quarter using your Priority Visa Infinite Credit Card. This is complimentary for all cardholders.
The Priority Pass programme provides you with 2 international lounge visits per month, subject to the cardholder meeting the FUM* (Funds under Management) qualification criteria of INR 30 Lakh at an individual account level, and the respective primary and supplementary credit card being active in the calendar month of the usage. Please note that the card member will be liable to pay for each domestic lounge visit using Priority Pass, as per applicable charges.
How to access Priority Pass Lounge benefits?
Your Priority Visa Infinite credit card offers you complimentary membership to Priority Pass, which gives you access to lounges in international airports across the globe. You can log on to www.prioritypass.com for details on the participating lounges. To know more on the terms and conditions, click here .

Fees and Charges
Primary Card:
The DigiSmart credit card has no annual fee. Instead, clients will be charged a modest sum of INR 49 per month. This is waived on monthly spends of INR 5000 in the next calendar month of fee levied.
Supplementary card:
Like in the case of the primary card, payment of an annual fee is not required. Charges only include INR 49 per month which is waived on monthly spends of INR 5000 in the next calendar month of fee levied.
DigiSmart Credit Card FAQs
What is a DigiSmart credit card?
DigiSmart Card is a newly launched best credit card for online shopping by Standard Chartered Bank. This credit card offers discounts and cashback on groceries, movies, fashion, cab rentals, food & travel spends made online.
What makes DigiSmart Credit Card the best credit card for online Shopping by Standard Chartered?
DigiSmart credit card has been specially customized for those who love online shopping. It provides exclusive discounts and offers on shopping with some of the major online brands across diverse segments. Some of these brands include Blinkit, Zomato, Yatra, among others. With online shopping on a consistent rise, it is safe to say this is a perfect credit card for online shopping ventures.
How to avail of the discounts and offers on this credit card for online shopping?
Before getting started, you can go through the various shopping benefits of using a DigiSmart card across diverse brands for an easy headstart. On shopping with Blinkit, Zomato and Yatra, use the promo code DIGISMART to avail of the discounts and offers, using this credit card. On shopping with Inox, and OLA, no promo code is required; you just have to simply checkout using your DigiSmart credit card.
I am already holding another SCB credit card? Can I still apply for a DigiSmart card?
Yes, existing SCB credit card owners can also apply for the DigiSmart card.
When will the monthly fees be charged?
The card fee will be billed every month starting with the first statement.
How will the fees be reversed on the primary card?
The monthly fee will be reversed by the bank within 90 days of fee levy if the spends on the primary card exceeds INR 5000 in the next calendar month of fee levy or as communicated by the bank from time to time.
E.g. If your January month statement carries a card usage fee of INR 49, you need to spend INR 5000 or more in February for the January month card usage fee to be reversed in your April credit card Statement.
Is the fees applicable on supplementary cards also?
Yes, the monthly fee of INR 49 is applicable on the supplementary card also.
How will the fees be reversed on the supplementary card?
The monthly fee will be reversed by the bank within 90 days of fee levy if the spends on the supplementary card exceeds INR 5000 in the next calendar month of fee levy or as communicated by the bank from time to time.
E.g. If your January month statement carries a card usage fee of INR 49, you need to spend INR 5000 or more in February for the January month card usage fee to be reversed in your April credit card Statement.
Is the Ola credit card offer applicable for all rides?
Yes, this offer on DigiSmart card is applicable for all cab rides except Ola bike, auto, and pool rides.
How to avail credit card movie offers (BOGO- Buy 1 Get 1) with INOX
You can avail BOGO movie offers on INOX with the following steps –
Visit www.inoxmovies.com/mobile app/mobile website
Go through the regular ticket booking flow for selecting the location, movie, show timings, and seat(s) of your choice
Confirm the details by providing your e-mail address and mobile number as this information will be used for confirming your transaction through e-mail and SMS respectively
Proceed to the Order Summary Page and select “Standard Chartered Bank – Buy 1 and get 1” on movie tickets from Discounts & Offers section
Enter details in the box and click on “Apply” button to avail the discount
Fill in your valid DigiSmart card details as required on payment page to proceed with the transaction
What is a Virtual DigiSmart Card and how to get one?
A virtual DigiSmart Card is the online replication of the physical DigiSmart card with a unique card number, CVV, and expiry date. It can be used instantly from anywhere across the globe without the need of the plastic card- you can simply store the virtual card details on your smartphone.
On issuing a DigiSmart card, you can easily apply for its virtual card too with a few, uncomplicated select-and-apply steps. On completing the process, you can access your virtual DigiSmart credit card for online shopping immediately with zero waiting time. To know more about the application process, click here .
This digital credit card is convenient for everyone who wish to explore DigiSmart card’s exciting perks immediately after applying for it- you don’t have to wait for the physical card to reach your doorsteps.
What merchant categories will be considered as rental payments?
Credit card transactions with Merchant Category Code (MCC) 6513 are covered under Rental Payments.
A Merchant Category Code (MCC) is a four-digit number assigned to a merchant/business by the merchant’s acquiring bank. These are defined by networks e.g. Visa, MasterCard.
What is the processing fee for rental payments?
A processing fee of 1% (plus taxes) would be applicable to all rental transactions. This fee will be over and above any charges applied by the merchant as communicated to the client at the time of transaction.
For example – If you pay INR 20,000 on a third-party rent payment platform using your Standard Chartered Bank Credit Card, 1% of transaction amount i.e. INR 200 plus taxes will be charged as a processing fee
When will this processing fee be effective from?
Processing fee on rental payments will be effective from April 02, 2023.
When will this fee get levied?
The processing fee will be levied by 15 th  of the next calendar month and will have a separate narration in the monthly statement.

FAQs
Which are the currencies that can be loaded on the card?
There are 20 currencies offered which can be loaded on the card, namely,
USD (U.S. Dollar)
EUR (Euro)
GBP (British Pound)
AED (UAE Dirhams)
AUD (Australian Dollar)
BHD (Bahraini Dinar)
CAD (Canadian Dollar)
CHF (Swiss Franc)
DKK (Danish Krone)
HKD (Hong Kong Dollar)
JPY (Japanese Yen)
KWD (Kuwaiti Dinar)
NOK (Norwegian Krone)
NZD (New Zealand Dollar)
OMR (Omani Rial)
QAR (Qatari Rial)
SEK (Swedish Krone)
SGD (Singapore Dollar)
THB (Thai Baht)
ZAR (South African Rand)
Can I make a transaction in any other currency apart from mentioned 20 currencies?
Yes. Transactions can be carried out anywhere across the globe using your Forex Card in any currency. If the transaction currency is different from the ones available in your card, the card will automatically search for the currency with sufficient balance using a bank-defined authorization order & process the transaction. A cross currency mark-up would be charged against the transaction amount.
Where can I apply for the Forex Card?
Forex Cards can be applied from any of the Standard Chartered branches across India. Alternatively, you can also reach out to your Relationship Manager to apply for a Forex Card.
Whom do I call in case I need any assistance?
You can contact Phone Banking or write to us or visit any nearest Standard Chartered branch. You can visit www.sc.bank.in/ for more details.
I want to make an insurance claim. What is the process?
You need to write a mail to bankclaims@hdfcergo.com with the details about the claim & they will help you out with the entire process & the documentation needed.
Do I need to activate the Forex Card for Ecommerce transactions?
The Forex Card is pre-activated to perform Ecommerce transactions once it is loaded with the currency required but you need to ensure that you have completed the first time login process for Customer Portal since the IPIN (Customer Portal password) is required as the second factor to authenticate such transactions.
What is IPIN?
IPIN is the Customer Portal password set by you. IPIN is required as a second factor to authenticate Ecommerce transactions.
I do not have a Savings bank account with Standard Chartered Bank. Can I apply for a Forex Card?
You can apply for a savings account by choosing any variant from our bouquet of offerings & once the account is activated, you can apply for a Forex Card.
Why have I received two cards in the Welcome Kit?
Forex Card kit comes with two Cards (Primary and Backup Card). In case your Primary Card is lost/damaged/stolen, you can hotlist the same, get your Backup Card activated & entire balance will get transferred instantly and automatically.
Why have I received two PINs in the Welcome Kit?
One PIN is for Primary Card & the other PIN is for Backup Card. The respective card numbers will be printed on the PINs.
Can I issue cheques to load the Forex Card?
No. Forex Card can be loaded only by debiting your Standard Chartered savings account.
What are the charges attached to the Forex Card?
Refer the Schedule of Service Charges (SOSC) available towards the end of this page.
If I am travelling to multiple countries, do I need to carry multiple cards?
Multi-Currency Forex card is a complete solution to all your Forex needs. This card can support up to 20 currencies. You don’t need to carry multiple cards for travelling to different countries. Same card can be used across the globe.
What are the authorization scenarios?
When you perform a payment transaction, the debit would take place on the priority as per the grid provided below,
Scenario 1 – If you have sufficient balance in local currency (depending on country of transaction) then the transaction will be processed using the local currency balances.
Scenario 2 – If the local currency is part of the offered multiple currencies on the card but you do not have sufficient balance in the local currency, however if the you have sufficient balance (equivalent to local currency) in default currency (USD) then the transaction will be processed using the balances in the default currency. Value from the default currency will be converted to local currency using cross currency conversion rate decided by the Bank. This rate will be calculated with a markup on the IBR cross currency rate.
Scenario 3 – If the local currency is part of the offered multiple currencies on the card but you do not have sufficient balance in local currency as well as default currency (USD) however if the you have sufficient balance (equivalent to local currency) in any other currency then the transaction will be processed using the balances from the respective currency. The card will look for balances across all the currencies in following order of priority as decided by the Bank,
1. USD
2. GBP
3. EUR
4. SGD
5. AUD
6. CAD
7. JPY
8. CHF
9. HKD
10. NZD
11. THB
12. ZAR
13. SEK
14. NOK
15. DKK
16. AED
17. BHD
18. KWD
19. OMR
20. QAR
Scenario 4 – If you transact in currency which is not offered on the card, the transaction will be processed by debiting default currency (USD). Cross currency conversion would take place as per applicable rates. In case there is no sufficient balance in default currency then funds would be pulled from other currency with sufficient funds and converted to the default currency using Bank’s currency conversion rate. Partial transactions in different currencies are not allowed. If you do not have sufficient balance to process the transactions individually in any of the currencies available on your card, the transaction is declined even if the collective balances on the card across all currencies is sufficient to process the transaction.
Can I retain the Forex card once in return to India?
You can retain the card only if the balance remaining on the card is less than $2000. Else the amount needs to be refunded within 180 days from the date of arrival. This is as per RBI guidelines.
How can I cancel the Forex Card?
To cancel your Forex Card, we request you visit your nearest Standard Chartered Bank branch and place a request towards cancellation of the card. Your request will be processed within one working day of us receiving the request and the available balance on the card will be refunded back to your account. There are no charges towards the cancellation of your Card. However, currency conversion charges as applicable from time to time will be deducted at the time of providing credit to you.
What is the maximum value for which the Forex Card can be used?
There is no limit imposed on the usage of your Forex Card at POS. You can utilise any amount, up to the value loaded on the Forex card at any POS terminal. There is $2000 (or equivalent in other currencies) daily limit on cash withdrawals. However, there are some ATM service providers which have put per day / per transaction limit towards cash dispense which cannot be changed by the Bank. In such cases, you might need to do multiple transactions if you want to withdraw any amount higher than that allowed in one transaction.
Is the Forex Card enabled for Verified by VISA (VBV)?
Yes. Forex Card, offered on VISA platform, is pre-enabled for Verified by VISA that allows you to securely perform Ecommerce transactions.
Following are the steps to perform Ecommerce transaction with VBV participating merchants:
Select the goods or services you want from a online store and proceed to the payment page
Enter your Standard Chartered Bank Multi-Currency Forex Card number and the online store will connect with Standard Chartered Bank to check whether your Card is secured with Verified by Visa
Enter your IPIN (used for login to Customer Portal) and Standard Chartered Bank will then confirm your identity to the merchant
Your payment is authorized and your order is placed
Can the value of the Forex Card be reloaded / topped up?
Yes. Your Forex Card can be reloaded multiple times in future till the card gets expired. If you are abroad then you can use the online reload facility through the customer portal. Alternatively, you can visit any Standard Chartered Bank branch in India & submit your reload request.
Can the Forex Card be reloaded over the Internet?
Yes. When you are abroad & have run out of balance on your forex card, you can submit a request via the customer portal to reload the card with the required currency & amount. The request will be processed within 1 working day.
What are the steps to reload online?
You can submit online reload request using the Customer Portal. Refer the “Convenience” feature in the page above for detailed steps.
Note: Above online reload request will be processed within 1 working day.
I cannot see few currencies while I try to submit reload request via Customer Portal. Why is that so?
You can see only those currencies on the customer portal which you had requested the branch to load on your card. You cannot add a new currency using Customer Portal & you will have to visit the branch for the same.
I have placed a request for Online Reload on Customer Portal but did not receive any SMS or Email alert. How do I know if the request has been placed or not?
Once you place the request, you will see an acknowledgement page. This is a confirmation that your request has been placed. Once the request is processed within 1 working day, you will receive a SMS & Email alert once the reload request is processed.
I'm not a traveller, can I still benefit from the Forex Card?
The Forex Card is only for travellers, and cannot be issued to non-travellers.
How can I avail of Global Customer Assistance Services (GCAS) for my Forex Card?
You can call the VISA call centre numbers to avail VISA’s Global Customer Assistance Services. You can find a country wise list of GCAS numbers on VISA website.
What are the charges towards availing GCAS services?
Following are the charges for availing GCAS facility offered by Visa:
Emergency Card Replacement: US $ 175
Emergency Cash Disbursement: US $ 175
Cardholder Inquiry Service: US $ 5 per inquiry
Lost/Stolen Card reporting: US $ 35 per card
What should I do and whom to contact if my card is lost?
In case your card gets lost or stolen, all you have to do is call Phone Banking immediately and report the loss of your card. This facility is available 24 hrs on all days. You can request Phone Banking to activate your backup card. Once the backup card is activated, all the funds from your primary card will be transferred automatically and instantly to the backup card.
What should I do if my Forex Card has expired but there is unutilised balance on the card?
If your Card has expired before all funds have been utilised, you can contact Phone Banking or any of our Standard Chartered Bank branch for placing a refund request of the unutilised balance. In case you wish you get a replacement card, you can place a request at any of our Standard Chartered Bank branch.
What if the balance in the currency of transaction goes down?
The card has inbuilt intelligence to process the transaction if there is sufficient fund in any of the available currencies to authorize the transaction. If there is no sufficient balance in the transaction currency, the payment is processed by debiting the currency with sufficient balance. Please note that if there is no sufficient balance in any currency then the transaction will be declined even if collectively, across the currencies, the card has sufficient balance. Please note that cross currency conversion charges will be applicable in cases where the transaction is processed by utilizing funds from different currency.
Who should I contact for my replacement card? Is there a charge for replacement?
You can place a replacement request either through your Relationship Manager or visit your nearest Standard Chartered branch. The charges for reissuance are given in schedule of charges.
How does the Forex Card work in Contactless mode?
Look for VISA payWave mark or Contactless logo at the point of purchase
To pay, place your card over the reader at close range (less than 4 cm from where the Contactless logo appears)
Once the reader confirms that your transaction is approved, you are ready to go. No signature or PIN required
What if I wave the card twice at the terminal? Will I be charged twice?
No. Even if the card is tapped / waved more than once at the terminal, the customer will only get billed once for the purchase.
How will I know if the transaction is successful?
You will receive a charge slip from the merchant, and also receive an Emailer / SMS on your registered contact details once the transaction is successful.
Is there any difference in the process for ATM and Internet transaction for this Contactless Card?
There is no difference in the transaction process for ATM & Internet transactions. For ATM transactions you need to enter the PIN and for Internet transaction you need to enter your 3D secure PIN.
How can I check the balance on my Forex Card?
You can check the balance on all the active currency wallets on Multi-Currency card through the customer portal. Alternately, you can also contact our Phone Banking services to check the balance on your Multi-Currency Card.
Are there any transactions for which this Card should not be used?
Yes. Your Forex Card should not be used towards temporary charge blocks – e.g. paying deposits at hotels, hiring of cars etc.
Do I need to pay additional amount over and above Cash withdrawal Charges levied by Standard Chartered Bank for ATM transaction?
In certain countries ATM Service providers are authorized to charge surcharge/convenience fees from the cardholders for accessing the ATM to withdraw money. These charges are levied by the ATM service provider over and above the charges levied by the Standard Chartered Bank, and Standard Chartered Bank has no control over the charges as these charges are not levied by Standard Chartered Bank. These charges are normally displayed to the customer during the transaction and the customer has the option to either proceed with the transaction or abort if the charges are not acceptable to the customer.
What are the exchange rates applicable when I use my Forex Card for transactions (Cash withdrawal at ATMs and point of sale transactions at Merchant Outlets)?
If the transaction is done in base currency (currency with which the card was loaded), there is no exchange rate applicable. However, for transactions other than the base currency, exchange rates / conversion rates from the base currency to the local currency would be applicable.
How can I change the ATM PIN of my Forex Card?
You can change the PIN using the Customer Portal. Refer the “Convenience” feature in the page above for detailed steps.
How can I view the transactions I did with my Forex Card?
You can view the transactions using the Customer Portal. Refer the “Convenience” feature in the page above for detailed steps.
How can I get the statement of my Forex Card?
You can get the statement using the Customer Portal. Refer the “Convenience” feature in the page above for detailed steps.
Can the Forex Card be temporarily blocked?
You can get the statement using the Customer Portal. Refer the “Convenience” feature in the page above for detailed steps.
My customer portal User ID is blocked. How do I unblock?
Use the “Forgot Your Password” link on the login screen of Customer Portal to change your password. This will unblock your User ID as well.
How does Multi-Currency Forex Card work?
Your Multi-Currency Forex Card will be activated with the required currencies instantly over the counter at the branch. Once active, the card can be used at any international location for making payments at POS terminals or to withdraw cash at ATMs. Usage of the card is not allowed in India, Nepal, Bhutan, Iran, Syria, North Korea, Cuba, Sudan.
Multi-Currency Forex Card has a chip & a radio frequency antenna embedded. When this Contactless Card is tapped/waved against the terminal enabled for contactless payments, the card transmits payment details wirelessly, eliminating the need to insert it. Account details are communicated to the reader and then processed through the secure Visa acceptance network in the usual way.
Multi-Currency Forex Card works just like any Debit / Credit Card for making payments at Merchant Establishments that have an electronic terminal. The transaction amount is deducted from available balance on the Multi-Currency Card. This card can be used at all Merchant Establishments displaying the VISA symbol.
This card can be used to withdraw cash at all VISA ATMs worldwide. Irrespective of the currency loaded on the card, the cash will be dispensed based on the currencies supported at the ATMs.
What is Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS)?
Under this Scheme, all resident individuals, including minors, are allowed to freely remit / avail foreign exchange facility up to USD 2,50,000 per financial year (April – March) for any permissible current or capital account transaction or a combination of both.

Examples on client exhausting the annual LRS limit and understanding its TCS applicability –
Considering Purpose of travel as Medical – If a client has utilised his LRS limit of 10 lakhs and has initiated a foreign exchange transaction for additional INR 3,00,000, his total utilisation for financial year would be INR 10,00,000, however TCS would be applicable only for additional 3 lakhs @ 5% for travel under medical purpose.
Considering Purpose of travel as Leisure – If a client has utilised his LRS limit of 10 lakhs and has initiated a foreign exchange transaction for additional INR 3,00,000, his total utilisation for financial year would be INR 10,00,000, however TCS would be applicable only for additional 3 lakhs @ 20% for travel under leisure purpose.
What is the tax implication if the amount remitted for pursuing overseas education is Owned Funds?
TCS at 5% shall be applicable on remittances exceeding INR 10 lakhs in a financial year.
Which transactions are included in threshold limit of INR 10 lakhs, above which TCS shall be applicable?
All forex transactions under LRS will be included in threshold limit of INR 10 lakhs.
Will TCS be applicable if foreign exchange facility is availed for Forex cards?
Yes, TCS will be applied on LRS transactions exceeding INR 10 lakhs if foreign exchange facility is availed for loading / reloading of Forex cards.
If remitter/ customer can avail tax credit of the TCS?
Yes, remitter / customer can claim credit for the tax collected by the Bank while filing for their tax returns.
Is TCS deduction on LRS transaction a regulatory requirement?
Yes, TCS collection on LRS transactions is a regulatory requirement. This is amended under 206C (1G) of the Income-tax Act, 1961.
In case of return of transactions post debit from the account, will the TCS get refunded back?
TCS will not be reversed once debited. Remitter / Client can approach for refund from tax authority through ITR.
Will GST be applied on the TCS amount on Forex Card?
GST will not be applicable on the TCS amount.
